title: Staff AI Engineer
company_focus:
Technology platform for reaching business-critical decision
makers
description:
Applecart is the leading technology company that C-suites
rely on to reach business-critical decision makers. The most
important decisions are made by the hardest people to reach
and influence. Historically, C-suite leaders have lacked the
tools to reach the stakeholders who matter most to their
business. The C-suites of hundreds of Fortune 500 companies,
major agencies, trade associations, nonprofits, and
governments use Applecart to put their best content in front
of business-critical decision makers and those they trust —
from policymakers and investors to CEOs, key employees,
members of the media and more. Decision makers are informed
by what they read, learn from advisors, hear from
colleagues, and discuss with family and friends. To break
through to them, you must reach them through the only
channel that really moves them: those they know and trust.
Applecart’s platform uses publicly available data to map
billions of social relationships between nearly every
American adult and enable clients to deliver content
directly to decision makers and those who matter most to
them.

We are hiring a Staff AI Engineer in our New York City
office.

About the Role:

The role is for the Identity team at Applecart. The team
builds and maintains best in class identity data products,
tying multiple systems and data sources together. As a Staff
AI Engineer, you will be responsible for leading the design
and deployment of our next-generation AI-powered platforms.
You will shape the technical destiny of our organization,
moving beyond individual features to architect the agentic
ecosystems that will handle our next phase of growth. This
role will report to the Director of Engineering.

What You’ll Do:

- Lead the design of end-to-end AI solutions using foundation
  models, multi-agent orchestration (MAS), and RAG
  pipelines. Ensure that today’s technology choices support
  long-term scalability.
- Identify systemic risks and infrastructure bottlenecks
  before they impact production and ensure the reliability,
  availability, and scalability of data pipelines and
  systems through effective monitoring, alerting, and
  incident management.
- Mentor other engineers. Provide design feedback that teaches
  thinking, recommends scalable abstractions, and builds a
  culture of technical excellence.
- Implement efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
  architectures and integrate with enterprise data
  infrastructure.
- Stay up to date with industry advancements in GenAI and
  apply modern technologies and methodologies to our
  systems.
- Work closely with the data infrastructure team to build a
  unified data management and processing platform using
  Apache Spark, Unity Catalog, Iceberg, Dagster, etc.

Experiences and Capabilities You Bring:

- You have 8+ years of professional software development
  experience, especially in the fields of data engineering
  and AI/ML
- Deep expertise in applied AI/ML techniques (Deep Learning,
  NLP, GNNs), technologies (Agentic AI, RAG, Prompt/Context
  Engineering), and fluency in Python or a similar backend
  language
- You have designed and implemented scalable data processing
  pipelines, and understand how to store and retrieve large
  sets of data
- You have experience implementing various data warehousing
  architectures, including products like Snowflake or
  Databricks
- You have proven records of leading AI/ML projects, ideally
  RAG applications
- Proven experience with agentic frameworks (LangGraph,
  CrewAI, AutoGen) and modern data infrastructure (Kafka,
  Spark, Vector Search).
- You are an excellent verbal and written communicator. You
  are an expert at producing visuals for technical
  communication.

Preferred qualifications:

- Hands-on experiences with graph data models and graph data
  processing (with graph data engines such as Neo4 or
  PuppyGraph) is a big plus.
- You have hands-on experience in implementing the Lakehouse
  architecture.
- You are familiar with identity resolution and have
  experience building identity resolution systems.
- You have experience with backend API designing and
  implementation
- You have experience optimizing the end to end performance of
  distributed systems
- Impeccable attention to detail and superior organizational
  skills

This is a hybrid role. Work from home on Monday and Friday
and in Applecart’s New York City office Tuesday through
Thursday.

Compensation:

Our compensation package consists of three components: (i) a
base salary, (ii) annual cash bonus, and (iii) generous
equity compensation.

The base salary range for this role is between $200,000 -
250,000 based on the candidate's experience and skills
relative to the requirements listed above. The salary range
provided is exclusive of discretionary annual cash bonus and
equity compensation. The salary range provided for this role
is specific to candidates located in the New York City area.

A few facts about us:

- Applecart’s platform has been used by hundreds of industry-
  leading clients spanning Fortune 500 companies like
  Blackstone and Intuit, leading nonprofit organizations
  like the Environmental Defense Fund, and top-tier
  advertising and communications agencies like Teneo, FGS,
  Weber Shandwick, and OMD.
- Applecart is growing quickly — over the last 4 years, we’ve
  increased our revenue by 10X, grown our headcount by
  almost 5X, and we’re cashflow positive.
- Our senior leadership team includes two members of the
  Forbes 30 under 30 list for Marketing and Advertising, the
  former Head of Product at Moat, Oracle’s online ad
  measurement platform, a former senior White House advisor,
  early sales and talent leaders from Google and Twitter,
  two of the Democratic Party’s most successful pollsters
  and strategists, and alums of top-tier organizations like
  Bain & Company, Amazon and Simpson Thacher.
- Applecart raised a $100M Series C from Blackstone’s tech
  growth fund at the end of 2025. The company’s other
  investors include Bain & Company, global sports,
  entertainment and marketing giant Endeavor, KKR Co-Founder
  Henry Kravis, entrepreneur Chris Burch, a founder of
  Palantir, senior engineering leaders at Twitter and Yelp,
  former Fortune 50 and advertising agency CEOs, and a
  number of well-known venture capitalists. Ari Emanuel, CEO
  of TKO and Executive Chairman of WME, Wayne Berman,
  Blackstone’s Head of Global Government Affairs, and Risa
  Heller, CEO of HELLER, serve on our board.
- Our work has been featured on Morning Joe on MSNBC,
  BloombergTV, and The Colbert Report, and in Axios,
  Semafor, BusinessWeek, the Associated Press, Forbes, the
  Washington Post, and Politico, among many others.
responsibilities: Lead the design of end-to-end AI solutions using foundation models, multi-agent orchestration (MAS), and RAG pipelines
Identify systemic risks and infrastructure bottlenecks before they impact production and ensure reliability, availability, and scalability of data pipelines and systems
Mentor other engineers and provide design feedback that builds a culture of technical excellence
Implement efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architectures and integrate with enterprise data infrastructure
Stay up to date with industry advancements in GenAI and apply modern technologies and methodologies to systems

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is hands-on building AI systems - leading design
of end-to-end AI solutions using foundation models, multi-
agent orchestration, and RAG pipelines. The engineer will
architect agentic ecosystems and implement efficient RAG
architectures, directly working with models and agents
rather than just supporting infrastructure.
